title: NCRPB Medical Attendance Scheme for Pensioners, 2005
description: NCRPB Medical Attendance Scheme for Pensioners, 2005
text: 1.3 This Scheme came into effect from 9.7. 2005. 1.2 The provisions of this Scheme herein contained shall apply to Group A, B, C & D penaoners of the Board and their dependants. 1.1 This Scheme will be called ''NCRPB Medical Attendance Scheme for Pensioners, 2005". Rs. l,760/- Group 'D' R.s.2, l 00/- Group 'C' Rs.2,900/- Group 'B' Rs.4,300/- Group 'A' Pensioners/Family Pensioners" Category 4.1 The annual ceiling for outdoor treatment for the pensioners shall be as under.- Section IV: 4.0 Monetary limits 3.3 The pensioners residing outside the NCT Delhi may get themselves/members of their families treated from the medical practitioners, possessing qualifications not less than MBBS and the cost of such treatment would be reimbursed to them subject to the approved ceiling. 3.2 Photo Identity Cards to the pensioners and their dependants will be issued by the Board on receipt of written request from them, which will be produced by the claimants at the time of taking treatment. (Text of the Identity Card is enclosed.) 3.1 The pensioner shall have to apply in writing for admission to the Scheme. Section-III 3.0 Provisions 2.2 For the purpose of this Scheme, the definition of family and dependants will be as given in the Central Services (Medical Attendance) Rules, 1944 as amended from time to time. 2.1 Benefits under this Scheme will be applicable to the pensioners of the Board, their families including their dependants. Section- II: 2.0 Membership- Eligibility of self and family:- 2963 THE GAZE1TE OF INDIA, DECEMBER 10, 2005 (AGRAHAYANA 19, 1927) PART Ill-Sa:. 4] -- 2 of 3 -- (P.K. MISHRA) Member Secretary 7.2 In cas any issue is not covered under this Scheme, the provisions of Central Services (Medi al Attendance) Rules, 1944 will be applicable and decisions/instructions thereu der shall apply to all pensioners of the Board. 7.1 The wers to interpret these Rules are reserved with the Member Secretary, NCR Planni g Board. Section VII: ) .Cardiac Surgery/ Cardiology ) Oncology ) Organ transplant ) Joint replacement ) Major Neuro-Surgical/ Neurology cases 6.2 Post rative OPD Treatment, towards reimbursement of cost of medicines will also be admis ible under the Scheme for the following diseases subject to the conditions laid down n Central Services (Medical Attendance) Rules. 1944:- 6.1 For in oor treatment, the pensioner will be entitled for same facilities as available to the· employees of the Board. Section VI: 6 0 Indoor medical facilities to ensioners ~ Cancer ) Diabetes ) Mental diseases ·) Poliomyelitis, Cerebral Palsy and Spastics ) Tubercular Diseases ) Leprosy ) Thalasaemia Major 5.1 In add tion to the above monetary limits for OPD treatment, the medical reimbursement will al o be admissible for treatment of following special diseases without taking into acco the overall monetary ceiling as laid down in para 4.1 above. However, the treatm nt for such diseases should be taken from Hospitals as mentioned in Central Servic s (Medical Attendance) Rules, 1944.
